content:
How Angular’s New Reactivity Actually Works (And How to Think in Signals)

4 min read
16 hours ago

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

Source — InternetAngular 17 and 18 didn’t just introduce a new API — they introduced a new way of thinking.
Signals are not “another state management tool.” They are Angular’s foundational reactivity primitive. If you try to compare them directly with RxJS or NgRx, you’ll miss the point. Signals change how change detection works, how components render, and how data flows through your application.
If Not a Member READ FROM HERE
Let’s build a proper mental model.
1. The Problem Signals Solve
Before Signals, Angular relied heavily on:

• Zone.js to detect async changes

• Global change detection tree traversal

• RxJS for reactive state patterns
The issue wasn’t that this didn’t work. It did. But it came with tradeoffs:

• Over-rendering components unnecessarily

• Complex RxJS chains for simple state

• Hard-to-predict performance behavior
Signals move Angular from “dirty checking everything” to “update exactly what changed.”
